title_en: Galileo releases Agent Control, a centralized guardrails platform for enterprise AI agents
title_zh: Galileo开源Agent Control：一套策略管住全栈AI Agent
summary_en: Galileo releases Agent Control, an open source control plane for governing AI agents at scale. AWS, CrewAI, and Glean are among the first partners.
intro_zh: Galileo 发布了开源控制平面 Agent Control，核心目标是让企业把 AI Agent 治理从“分散硬编码”升级为“集中策略管控”。它支持一次定义 guardrails、跨多 Agent 运行时统一执行，并可实时更新策略而无需停机改代码。文章还梳理了其合作生态、典型落地场景以及与同类产品的竞争位置。
content_en_preview:
[Galileo](https://galileo.ai/), a company known for its AI observability and guardrails technology, on Wednesday released [Agent Control](https://github.com/agentcontrol/agent-control), an open source control plane designed to help enterprises govern AI agents at scale.

The platform allows organizations to write behavioral policies once and enforce them across all agent deployments.

AWS, CrewAI, and Glean will be among the first partners to offer Agent Control. This will give their customers access to centralized governance frameworks for managing agent behavior in production.

There’s certainly a growing market for this technology. According to [IDC, use of AI agents among Global 2000 organizations is expected to increase tenfold by 2027](https://www.idc.com/resource-center/blog/agent-adoption-the-it-industrys-next-great-inflection-point/). This, in turn, means token and API call volumes will spike by a factor of 1,000.

[Tim Law](https://www.linkedin.com/in/tim-law-airesearch/), IDC’s Research Director for AI and Automation, says in a press release about the news, “Centralized management of policies can help organizations manage AI agent behaviors. A unified control plane and centralized governance can help deploy AI agents at scale while ensuring continual improvement through evaluation and lifecycle management.”

What Agent Control brings to the table is a standardized way for companies to apply guardrails across agents, enabling:

*   A centralized policy layer for enforcing governance and blocking unsafe behaviors at runtime.
*   Real-time updates to agent policies without requiring downtime or code modifications.

[Vikram Chatterji](https://www.linkedin.com/in/vikram-chatterji), Galileo’s co-founder and CEO, tells _The New Stack_ that in practice, this means instead of using hard-coded, brittle safety rules, “Agent Control lets developers define guardrails once and apply them everywhere. By open-sourcing this under Apache 2.0, we’re ensuring every enterpris
content_zh_preview:
企业在大规模部署 AI Agent 时，真正的瓶颈已不再只是模型能力，而是如何统一治理：怎么把安全、合规、成本和行为一致性规则，稳定地应用到所有 Agent。Galileo 最新发布的开源项目 [Agent Control](https://github.com/agentcontrol/agent-control)，就是为这个问题提供“集中控制平面”方案。

[Galileo](https://galileo.ai/) 过去以 AI 可观测性与 guardrails 能力见长，这次发布的 Agent Control 采用 Apache-2.0 许可，目标是让企业“写一次策略，在所有 Agent 部署中执行”。

## Agent Control 到底解决什么问题？

在很多团队里，Agent 安全规则仍以硬编码方式散落在不同服务中：

- 规则难统一，跨团队标准不一致
- 更新策略要改代码、发版，响应慢
- 多框架/多供应商并存时，治理体系容易割裂

Agent Control 给出的做法是：把策略抽到统一控制层，运行时对 Agent 行为执行治理与拦截。

## 核心能力：集中策略 + 实时生效

Galileo 强调了两项关键能力：

- **集中策略层**：统一执行治理规则，在运行时拦截不安全行为。
- **实时策略更新**：无需停机、无需修改业务代码即可更新策略。

Galileo 联合创始人兼 CEO [Vikram Chatterji](https://www.linkedin.com/in/vikram-chatterji) 的表述很直接：不再依赖脆弱的硬编码安全规则，而是“一次定义，到处应用”。

> “Agent Control lets developers define guardrails once and apply them everywhere. By open-sourcing this under Apache 2.0, we’re ensuring every enterprise and developer community can use it without vendor lock-in.”  
> — Vikram Chatterji, co-founder and CEO, Galileo

## 生态与兼容性：强调“可插拔、可移植”

Galileo 表示，Agent Control 可对接任意 Agent，并支持来自任意厂商或企业自研工具的 guardrail evaluator。策略可跨环境移植，便于团队在 Agent 技术栈演进时保持治理连续性。

首批合作方包括 AWS、CrewAI 和 Glean，这意味着其客户可直接接入集中治理框架，在生产环境统一管理 Agent 行为。

## 典型企业场景

Galileo 预期的高频用例包括：

- 降低大语言模型 (LLM) 幻觉风险
- 执行数据隐私与合规规则
- 通过模型路由控制 Token 成本
- 保持面向客户的 Agent 语气一致性

## 为什么现在值得关注？

根据 IDC 预测，到 2027 年，Global 2000 企业中的 AI Agent 使用将增长 10 倍，对应 Token 与 API 调用量可能放大到 1,000 倍。

这意味着：如果没有统一控制平面，治理复杂度会先于业务价值失控。IDC AI 与自动化研究总监 [Tim Law](https://www.linkedin.com/in/tim-law-airesearch/) 也强调，集中策略管理与统一控制平面是企业规模化部署 Agent 的关键。

## 竞品格局：赛道已拥挤

Agent Control 并非唯一方案。文中提到的同类产品包括：

- [Humanlayer Agent Control Plane](https://github.com/humanlayer/agentcontrolplane)
- [GitHub Enterprise AI Controls](https://docs.github.com/copilot/concepts/agents/enterprise-management)
- [Microsoft Agent 365](https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/microsoft-agent-365)

不过，Galileo 已有大量 AI 团队（含 Fortune 50 企业）在使用其测试、监控和安全能力，这为其切入 Agent control plane 赛

title_en: Autonomous context compression
title_zh: Deep Agents新能力：让Agent自主压缩上下文
summary_en:
TL;DR: We've added a tool to the Deep Agents SDK (Python) and CLI that allows models to compress their own context windows at opportune times.


Motivation

Context compression is an action that reduces the information in an agent’s working memory. Older messages are replaced by a summary or condens
intro_zh: 这篇文章介绍了 Deep Agents 在 SDK 与 CLI 中新增的“自主上下文压缩”能力：把何时 compact 的决策权从固定阈值规则，交给模型自己判断。核心结论是，模型在任务边界、信息提炼后、长流程开始前等时机会更合理地触发压缩，减少上下文窗口耗尽与 context rot。读完你可以直接上手在 `deepagents` 中启用该中间件，并理解它适用的触发场景与实际效果。
content_en_preview:
![Autonomous context compression](https://blog.langchain.com/content/images/size/w760/format/webp/2026/03/Screenshot-2026-03-06-at-3.40.34---PM.png)

4 min read Mar 11, 2026

_TL;DR: We've added a tool to the_ [_Deep Agents SDK_](https://docs.langchain.com/oss/python/deepagents/overview?ref=blog.langchain.com) _(Python) and_ [_CLI_](https://docs.langchain.com/oss/python/deepagents/cli/overview?ref=blog.langchain.com) _that allows models to compress their own context windows at opportune times._

* * *

## Motivation

[Context compression](https://blog.langchain.com/context-management-for-deepagents/) is an action that reduces the information in an agent’s working memory. Older messages are replaced by a summary or condensed representation of an agent’s progress that preserves what’s relevant to a task. This action is often necessary to accommodate finite context windows and reduce [context rot](https://research.trychroma.com/context-rot?ref=blog.langchain.com).

Agent harnesses often control this by compacting at a fixed token threshold (`deepagents` uses [model profiles](https://docs.langchain.com/oss/python/langchain/models?ref=blog.langchain.com#model-profiles) to compact at 85% of any given model’s context limit). This design is suboptimal because there are good times and bad times to compact:

*   It is not ideal to compact when you’re in the middle of a complex refactor;
*   It is better to compact when you are starting a new task or otherwise believe that prior context will lose relevance.

Many interactive coding tools feature a `/compact` command or similar, which allows users to manually trigger a context compression step at opportune times. We take this one step further in the latest release of `deepagents`and expose a tool to the agent that lets it trigger context compression itself. This enables more opportunistic compaction without requiring your application’s users to be aware of finite context windows or issue specific commands.

This tool is current
content_zh_preview:
这项更新的核心是：Deep Agents 现在允许 Agent 在合适时机自行触发上下文压缩（context compression），而不是等到上下文窗口快满时才被动压缩。它解决的是长会话中常见的两个问题：有限上下文窗口，以及上下文老化（context rot）导致的推理质量下降。

![Autonomous context compression](https://blog.langchain.com/content/images/size/w760/format/webp/2026/03/Screenshot-2026-03-06-at-3.40.34---PM.png)

4 min read Mar 11, 2026

_TL;DR: We've added a tool to the_ [_Deep Agents SDK_](https://docs.langchain.com/oss/python/deepagents/overview?ref=blog.langchain.com) _(Python) and_ [_CLI_](https://docs.langchain.com/oss/python/deepagents/cli/overview?ref=blog.langchain.com) _that allows models to compress their own context windows at opportune times._

* * *

## 为什么要做“自主压缩”

[Context compression](https://blog.langchain.com/context-management-for-deepagents/) 的本质，是减少 Agent 工作记忆中的信息量：把较早消息替换为摘要，但保留任务相关内容。这样可以适配有限上下文窗口，并缓解 [context rot](https://research.trychroma.com/context-rot?ref=blog.langchain.com)。

传统做法通常是“到阈值就压缩”。例如 `deepagents` 会基于 [model profiles](https://docs.langchain.com/oss/python/langchain/models?ref=blog.langchain.com#model-profiles) 在模型上下文上限的 85% 触发 compact。

问题在于：压缩时机有好坏之分。复杂重构做到一半时压缩，往往会打断流程；而在任务刚完成、准备切换新任务时压缩，通常更合适。

很多 coding 工具已经有 `/compact` 这类手动命令。Deep Agents 这次更进一步：把压缩工具暴露给 Agent，让模型自主决定何时触发。这样用户不用理解上下文窗口细节，也不用手动下命令。

目前该能力在 [Deep Agents CLI](https://docs.langchain.com/oss/python/deepagents/cli/overview?ref=blog.langchain.com) 默认可用，在 `deepagents` SDK 中为 opt-in。

## 哪些时机适合 compact

文章给出了几类高价值触发点：

### 1）明确任务边界

- 用户表示要切换到新任务，旧上下文相关性下降
- Agent 交付完成，用户确认任务结束

### 2）从大量上下文中提炼出结果后

- Agent 已经从大段材料中提取出结论、事实或摘要（典型如 research 任务）

### 3）即将引入大量新上下文前

- 准备生成长文草稿
- 准备读取大量新资料

### 4）进入复杂多步骤流程前

- 即将开始长重构、迁移、多文件编辑、故障响应
- 计划已产出，准备执行步骤

### 5）新决策已覆盖旧前提

- 新需求出现，旧上下文部分失效
- 产生了大量支线和死胡同，可压缩为摘要

作者的观察是：人和大语言模型 (LLM) 都能识别这些“好时机”，提前 compact 比“窗口快满再压”更顺畅。模型侧的指导可见其 [system prompt](https://github.com/langchain-ai/deepagents/blob/537ed6cf153f9f6e50546c9d8674c32587540942/libs/deepagents/deepagents/middleware/summarization.py?ref=blog.langc

title_en: The Anatomy of an Agent Harness
title_zh: 拆解 Agent Harness：8个核心组件与设计逻辑
summary_en:
By Vivek Trivedy

intro_zh: 这篇文章提出一个非常实用的框架：Agent = Model + Harness，并把“模型之外的一切”系统能力统一归为 Harness。作者从“模型天然做不到什么”出发，反推为什么需要文件系统、代码执行、沙箱、记忆与上下文治理等能力。对国内开发者来说，核心启发是：Agent 能否稳定交付，关键不只在模型，而在 Harness engineering 的系统设计。
content_en_preview:
_By Vivek Trivedy_

**TLDR:** Agent = Model + Harness. Harness engineering is how we build systems around models to turn them into work engines. The model contains the intelligence and the harness makes that intelligence useful. We define what a harness is and derive the core components today's and tomorrow's agents need.

## Can Someone Please Define a "Harness"?

Agent = Model + Harness

**If you're not the model, you're the harness.**

A harness is every piece of code, configuration, and execution logic that isn't the model itself. A raw model is not an agent. But it becomes one when a harness gives it things like state, tool execution, feedback loops, and enforceable constraints.

Concretely, a harness includes things like:

*   System Prompts
*   Tools, Skills, MCPs + and their descriptions
*   Bundled Infrastructure (filesystem, sandbox, browser)
*   Orchestration Logic (subagent spawning, handoffs, model routing)
*   Hooks/Middleware for deterministic execution (compaction, continuation, lint checks)

There are many messy ways to split the boundaries of an agent system between the model and the harness. But in my opinion, this is the cleanest definition because it forces us to think about **designing systems around model intelligence.**

The rest of this post walks through core harness components and derives _why_ each piece exists working backwards from the core primitive of a model.

![](https://blog.langchain.com/content/images/2026/03/image.png)

## Why Do We Need Harnesses…From a Model's Perspective

**There are things we want an agent to do that a model cannot do out of the box. This is where a harness comes in.**Models (mostly) take in data like text, images, audio, video and they output text. That's it. Out of the box they cannot:

*   Maintain durable state across interactions
*   Execute code
*   Access realtime knowledge
*   Setup environments and install packages to complete work

These are all **harness level features**. The structure of LLMs req
content_zh_preview:
_By Vivek Trivedy_

**TLDR：** Agent = Model + Harness。Harness engineering 的本质，是围绕模型搭建一整套系统，让智能真正转化为可执行的工作能力。本文先定义什么是 Harness，再从模型原语出发推导当下与下一代 Agent 的核心 Harness 组件。

## 先把概念讲清：什么是 Harness？

Agent = Model + Harness

**如果你做的不是模型本身，那你大概率就在做 Harness。**

Harness 指的是：除模型外，所有代码、配置和执行逻辑。裸模型不是 Agent；只有当它被赋予状态、工具执行、反馈回路和可强制约束后，才会变成可工作的 Agent。

一个 Harness 通常包含：

* System Prompts
* Tools、Skills、MCP 及其描述
* 打包基础设施（filesystem、sandbox、browser）
* 编排逻辑（子 Agent 生成、handoff、模型路由）
* 保证确定性执行的 Hooks/Middleware（compaction、continuation、lint checks）

这一定义的价值在于，它逼着我们把注意力放在“如何围绕模型智能做系统设计”上，而不是只盯着模型参数本身。

后文会按“能力缺口 → Harness 设计”这条线展开。

![](https://blog.langchain.com/content/images/2026/03/image.png)

## 为什么必须有 Harness：从模型视角看能力缺口

很多我们希望 Agent 完成的事情，模型开箱并不能做。

模型（多数情况下）只是在输入文本/图像/音频/视频后输出文本。仅靠这一点，无法直接做到：

* 跨交互维持持久状态
* 执行代码
* 获取实时知识
* 搭建环境并安装依赖

这些都属于 **Harness 层能力**。

最常见例子是“聊天体验”：我们会在外层包一个 while loop，持续拼接历史消息与新输入。你每天在用的聊天产品，本质上都已经有了最基础的 Harness。

核心方法是：把期望的 Agent 行为，转成 Harness 的具体功能。

## 设计方法：从“想要的行为”反推 Harness 工程

Harness engineering 的作用，是把人类的先验和约束注入系统，帮助模型更稳定地完成任务。

随着模型能力增强，Harness 也在承担“外科手术式增强”：补齐模型原生做不到的能力，并修正其执行偏差。

本文不追求穷举功能清单，而采用统一推导模式：

**目标行为（或待修复问题） → 对应 Harness 设计。**

![](https://blog.langchain.com/content/images/2026/03/image-1.png)

## 文件系统：持久存储与上下文管理的底座

**目标：让 Agent 能接入真实数据、卸载上下文压力，并跨会话保存工作。**

模型只能直接处理上下文窗口内的信息。没有文件系统时，用户只能复制粘贴内容给模型，体验差，也不适合自主 Agent。

因此自然演化出的方案是：

**Harness 内置文件系统抽象与 fs-ops 工具。**

它带来几项基础能力：

* Agent 有了工作区，可读写数据、代码、文档。
* 信息可增量写入/卸载，不必全塞进上下文。
* 中间结果可持久化，状态可跨会话延续。
* 文件系统成为天然协作面：多 Agent 与人类可通过共享文件协同，Agent Teams 依赖这一点。

再叠加 Git 后，Agent 还能做版本跟踪、错误回滚和分支实验。

## Bash + 代码执行：通用工具能力

**目标：让 Agent 自主解题，而不是让人预先定义所有工具。**

当前主流执行范式是 [ReAct loop](https://docs.langchain.com/oss/python/langchain/agents?ref=blog.langchain.com)：模型思考→调用工具→观察结果→循环迭代。

问题在于，Harness 只能执行“已接线”的工具。若每个动作都要人工造工具，成本过高。

更优解是提供通用工具：bash。

**Harness 内置 bash 工具后，模型可以通过“写代码+执行代码”自主构造解法。**

这相当于给模型一台“可操作的计算机”，让它动态生成所需工具，而不被固定

title_en: How Coding Agents Are Reshaping Engineering, Product and Design
title_zh: Coding Agent重塑EPD：PRD已死，评审为王
summary_en: EPD (Engineering, Product, and Design) at software company is about creating good software. Separate roles exist, but the end goal is functional software that solves a business problem that users can use. At the end of the day, this is just code. It is important to recognize that the output of what
intro_zh: 这篇文章讨论了 Coding Agent 普及后，软件团队 EPD（工程、产品、设计）协作方式的根本变化：实现成本骤降，评审与系统思维成为新瓶颈。对中国开发者来说，核心启发是别再把精力只放在“写代码更快”，而要升级到“做对产品、审对架构、跨职能协作”。一句话总结：未来团队更看重两类人——能端到端交付的 builder，以及高质量把关的 reviewer。
content_en_preview:
EPD (Engineering, Product, and Design) at software company is about creating good software. Separate roles exist, but the end goal is functional software that solves a business problem that users can use. At the end of the day, this is just code. It is important to recognize that the output of what EPD as a function builds is just code because… coding agents have suddenly made code very easy to write. So how does this change the role of EPD?

**The changing process:**

*   PRDs are dead
*   The bottleneck shifts from implementation to review
*   Long live PRDs

**Impact on roles:**

*   Generalists are more valuable than ever
*   Coding agents are a requirement
*   Good PMs are great, bad PMs are terrible
*   Everyone needs product sense
*   The bar for specialization is higher
*   You're either a builder or a reviewer
*   Everyone thinks their role is most advantaged by coding agents - and they are right

## PRDs are dead

PRDs (Product Requirement Documents) were the focal point of building software in the pre-Claude era. The EPD process was largely:

*   Someone (usually product) has an idea
*   Product writes a PRD
*   Design takes PRD and creates a mock
*   Engineering turns mock into code

![](https://blog.langchain.com/content/images/2026/03/idea-to-code-flow--3--1.png)

[](https://x.com/hwchase17/article/2031051115169808685/media/2031049557803773952?ref=blog.langchain.com)This wasn’t a hard and fast rule (at startups these steps blended together, the best builders were able to do multiple of these together) but it was the textbook way to build things.

This was required because building the software (and building the mock) required a significant amount of time and effort. So disciplines were created to specialize in those efforts. As people became more specialized, there then became a need to communicate across those disciplines. The PRD was the basis of that, which kickstarted everything. It would then waterfall to design, who would turn pretty words into a
content_zh_preview:
EPD（工程、产品、设计）的共同目标一直没变：做出能解决业务问题、用户可用的软件。

变化在于，Coding Agent 把“把想法写成代码”这件事的门槛和成本拉低了。于是问题变成：当代码变得便宜，EPD 的核心价值在哪里？

## 流程变化：不是不要需求，而是不要旧流程

**变化主线：**

* PRD 不再是起点
* 瓶颈从实现转向评审
* 需求文档仍然重要，但形态会变

---

## PRD 已死：旧的线性协作正在失效

在前 Claude 时代，典型流程是：

* 产品提出想法
* Product 写 PRD（Product Requirement Document）
* Design 出 mock
* Engineering 实现为代码

![](https://blog.langchain.com/content/images/2026/03/idea-to-code-flow--3--1.png)

[](https://x.com/hwchase17/article/2031051115169808685/media/2031049557803773952?ref=blog.langchain.com)这个流程过去合理，因为“做出可运行软件”本身很贵，必须高度分工，再靠 PRD 对齐上下游。

现在不同了：Coding Agent 可以直接把想法快速变成可运行原型。所谓“PRD 已死”，本质上是“以 PRD 为起点的瀑布式流程已死”。

## 新瓶颈：实现变便宜，评审变稀缺

今天几乎所有人都能产出代码，但“能跑”不等于“能上线”。

EPD 的价值重心转向评审与裁决，主要看三件事：

* 工程视角：架构是否可扩展、可靠、性能达标
* 产品视角：是否真解决用户痛点
* 设计视角：交互是否直观、易用

因为初版代码成本极低，团队会看到更多原型涌入评审队列。

![](https://blog.langchain.com/content/images/2026/03/Group-2147239064.png)

[](https://x.com/hwchase17/article/2031051115169808685/media/2031049616851177472?ref=blog.langchain.com)以前项目数量受实现速度限制；现在项目数量受评审能力限制。瓶颈已经明确迁移。

## PRD 复活：文档仍必要，但要服务“评审”

旧流程消失，不代表需求描述不重要。

当某人快速做出原型后，要进入生产仍需 EPD 评审。评审者必须知道：某段行为是“有意设计”还是“Agent 偶然生成”。没有意图说明，评审效率会极低。

所以未来更合理的做法是：

* 原型先行
* 附带需求说明（可简可繁）
* 再进入跨职能评审

文档可以是传统 PRD，也可能是结构化、可版本化的 prompts。

![](https://blog.langchain.com/content/images/2026/03/Group-2147239066--1--1.png)

## 角色重估：谁在升值，谁会被淘汰

### 1）通才更值钱

能同时理解产品、设计、工程的人，本来就稀缺；在 Coding Agent 时代更稀缺。

原因很直接：沟通是最大成本。一个能跨三职能的人，常常比三人串行协作更快。

### 2）会用 Coding Agent 已是基本要求

* PM 可直接做原型验证，而不是只写 spec 等排期
* 设计师可在代码中迭代，不只停留在 Figma
* 工程师可把时间从实现转向系统设计

不会用的人，不是“保守”，而是会在产能上被替代。

### 3）好 PM 与差 PM 的差距会放大

好产品判断能快速产出高价值功能；差判断会更快制造“垃圾原型”，占用评审带宽，并借“都做出来了”推动错误上线，导致产品臃肿。

![](https://blog.langchain.com/content/images/2026/03/pm-value-chart-1.png)

### 4）系统思维成为核心竞争力

当执行成本下降，区分度来自“是否做对”。

* 工程：服务、API、数据库架构的整体建模能力
* 产品：识别用户真实需求而非表层诉求
* 设计：理解交互“为什么好用”的底层机制

系统思维既决定前期方向，也决定后期评审质量。

### 5）所有人都需要产品感

Agent 只能执行指令，不能替你定义正确目标。目标错了，产能越高

title_en: The era of “AI as text” is over. Execution is the new interface.
title_zh: Copilot SDK落地指南：执行型AI成新入口
summary_en: See how the GitHub Copilot SDK enables agentic workflows directly inside your applications.
intro_zh: 这篇文章聚焦一个关键转变：AI 不再只是“输入提示词、输出文本”，而是直接在应用内执行多步任务。GitHub Copilot SDK 把 Copilot CLI 的规划与执行能力开放为可编程层，让团队无需自建编排系统就能接入 Agent 工作流。对中国开发者而言，核心价值在于三种可复用落地模式：多步委派、MCP 上下文接入、IDE 外嵌入执行。
content_en_preview:
Over the past two years, most teams have interacted with AI the same way: provide text input, receive text output, and manually decide what to do next.

But production software doesn’t operate on isolated exchanges. Real systems execute. They plan steps, invoke tools, modify files, recover from errors, and adapt under constraints you define.

As a developer, you’ve gotten used to using GitHub Copilot as your trusted AI in the IDE. But I bet you’ve thought more than once: “Why can’t I use this kind of agentic workflow inside my own apps too?”

Now you can.

The [GitHub Copilot SDK](https://github.com/github/copilot-sdk/?utm_source=github-blog&utm_medium=referral&utm_campaign=2026-em-march-9) makes that execution layer available as a programmable capability inside your software.

Instead of maintaining your own orchestration stack, you can **embed the same production-tested planning and execution engine that powers GitHub Copilot CLI** directly into your systems.

If your application can trigger logic, it can now trigger agentic execution. This shift changes the architecture of AI-powered systems.

So how does it work? Here are three concrete patterns teams are using to embed agentic execution into real applications.

## Pattern #1: Delegate multi-step work to agents

For years, teams have relied on scripts and glue code to automate repetitive tasks. But the moment a workflow depends on context, changes shape mid-run, or requires error recovery, scripts become brittle. You either hard-code edge cases, or start building a homegrown orchestration layer.

With the Copilot SDK, your application can delegate intent rather than encode fixed steps.

For example:

Your app exposes an action like “Prepare this repository for release.”

Instead of defining every step manually, you pass intent and constraints. The agent:

*   Explores the repository
*   Plans required steps
*   Modifies files
*   Runs commands
*   Adapts if something fails

All while operating within defined bou
content_zh_preview:
过去两年，多数团队使用 AI 的方式都很类似：输入文本、获得文本输出，再由人决定下一步。

但生产级软件不是靠一问一答运转的。真实系统需要执行：规划步骤、调用工具、修改文件、处理错误，并在你设定的约束下动态调整。

很多开发者已经习惯在 IDE 里使用 GitHub Copilot，也常会问一个问题：能不能把这种 Agent 工作流直接放进自己的应用？

现在可以了。

[GitHub Copilot SDK](https://github.com/github/copilot-sdk/?utm_source=github-blog&utm_medium=referral&utm_campaign=2026-em-march-9) 把这层执行能力开放为可编程能力。你不必再维护一整套自建编排栈，就能把 GitHub Copilot CLI 背后的生产级规划与执行引擎嵌入到系统里。

只要你的应用能触发业务逻辑，它就能触发 Agent 执行。这会直接改变 AI 系统的架构方式。

## 三种可落地的执行型模式

下面是团队正在使用的三种典型模式。

### 模式一：把多步骤任务委派给 Agent

过去，团队常用脚本和胶水代码自动化重复任务。但一旦流程依赖上下文、执行中途变化，或需要错误恢复，脚本就会变脆。要么硬编码大量边界条件，要么走向自建编排系统。

使用 Copilot SDK 后，应用可以“委派意图”，而不是“写死步骤”。

例如，你的应用提供一个动作：“为这个仓库准备发布”。

你不需要手动定义所有步骤，只需传入目标和约束，Agent 会：

*   浏览仓库
*   规划必要步骤
*   修改文件
*   运行命令
*   失败时自适应调整

并且全过程都在设定边界内执行。

**价值**：系统规模扩大后，固定流程很容易失效。Agent 执行能在可观测、可约束前提下保持适应性，避免每次都重造编排框架。

[**View multi-step execution examples →**](https://github.com/github/awesome-copilot/tree/main/cookbook/copilot-sdk/?utm_source=github-blog&utm_medium=referral&utm_campaign=2026-em-march-9)

### 模式二：用结构化运行时上下文替代“全塞进 Prompt”

很多团队试图把更多逻辑写进提示词，但这会让流程越来越难测试、难推理、难演进。长期看，Prompt 会变成结构化系统集成的替代品，稳定性也会下降。

在 Copilot SDK 中，上下文可以结构化、可组合：

*   定义领域工具或 Agent 技能
*   通过 [Model Context Protocol (MCP)](https://github.com/microsoft/mcp-for-beginners/?utm_source=github-blog&utm_medium=referral&utm_campaign=2026-em-march-9) 暴露工具
*   让执行引擎在运行时按需拉取上下文

相比把归属信息、API Schema、依赖规则硬塞进 Prompt，Agent 可以在规划和执行阶段直接访问真实系统。

例如，一个内部 Agent 可以：

*   查询服务归属
*   拉取历史决策记录
*   检查依赖图
*   引用内部 API
*   在安全约束下执行动作

**价值**：可靠的 AI 工作流依赖“结构化且有权限控制”的上下文。MCP 提供了把 Agent 执行锚定在真实工具和真实数据上的基础设施。

### 模式三：把执行能力嵌入 IDE 之外

title_en: How context rot drags down AI and LLM results for enterprises, and how to fix it
title_zh: 企业AI失准元凶：Context Rot治理全解
summary_en: Context rot is undermining enterprise AI and LLM performance. Elastic's Abhimanyu Anand and analyst James Kobielus explain what it is and how to fix it.
intro_zh: 这篇文章聚焦企业在落地大语言模型 (LLM) 和 Agent 时常见却隐蔽的问题：Context Rot（上下文腐化）。它解释了为什么“数据越多越好”在AI时代会失效，以及Context Rot如何导致延迟、幻觉和推理能力下降。对中国开发者和AI平台团队来说，关键结论是：把上下文治理、评估指标与RAG检索策略前置，才能稳定模型效果。
content_en_preview:
One of the most quoted business mantras today states that if a company wants to be successful, _data is everything_. This has been repeated for decades, but with the furious expansion and broad use of AI and AI agents, it may no longer be true.

Instead, companies are learning every day that while data _is_ still important, the _context_ of that data may be even more critical when using AI.

So, what changed?

As AI gained steam, unintended consequences started throwing wrenches into the works, including the vexing problem of “context rot,” which is when giant pools of critical data are amassed for use with AI. And for enterprises and data scientists, this context rot has been causing major challenges.

## What is “context rot?”

It turns out that sometimes you _can_ have too much of a good thing, particularly when bringing huge amounts of new data into AI agents and [Large Language Models](https://thenewstack.io/llm/) (LLMs).

The problem is that as more fresh data is added, it can be at odds with the older data already there, diluting the results, introducing conflicting context, and causing agents and LLMs to become confused and lethargic. This context rot spoils the delicate brew that yields quality, trusted AI results. [Context relevance turns out to be central](https://www.elastic.co/search-labs/blog/context-engineering-relevance-ai-agents-elasticsearch) to successfully using massive pools of data with AI, agentic search, and hybrid search.

Because new data is brought in without removing out-of-date data, the LLMs processing it reach a saturation point when their “attention budgets” are exhausted. They become overloaded and simply cannot process every new piece of data they are receiving. The models then lose focus and derail their own reasoning, which defeats the strengths and speed of AI. The practice of context engineering was born from these needs to help enterprises learn to manage a model’s limited attention.

For business users, this serious disruption
content_zh_preview:
过去企业常说“数据就是一切”，但在AI应用尤其是大语言模型 (LLM) 与Agent场景里，这句话已经不完整了。现在更关键的是：**数据所处的上下文是否准确、及时、相关**。

当企业把越来越多数据接入模型时，一个高频风险开始出现——**Context Rot（上下文腐化）**。它会直接拉低AI系统的可用性与可信度。

## 什么是Context Rot？

Context Rot并不是单纯“数据不够”，而是“数据太多且质量不受控”。

随着新数据不断加入，如果旧数据没有及时清理，就会出现：
- 新旧信息冲突
- 低相关内容稀释关键信号
- 上下文窗口被无效内容占满

结果是模型的注意力预算（attention budget）被耗尽，LLM难以聚焦核心事实，推理链路被打断，输出质量明显下降。

这也是为什么“Context engineering”会成为企业AI落地中的核心能力：它本质上是在有限Token与窗口内，最大化上下文有效密度。

## 在企业里，问题通常怎么暴露？

Elastic高级数据科学家Abhimanyu Anand指出，Context Rot在生产环境里常见几类症状：

- Agent反复循环调用工具，迟迟无法收敛
- 单次任务响应时间显著变长
- 检索了大量文档后仍找不到答案
- 最终出现幻觉，给出看似完整但错误的结果

他强调，即使是当前最强LLM，在超大上下文下也会出现推理能力下降。Token越多，不等于效果越好；无差别灌入数据只会加剧稀释效应。

## 修复思路：先监控，再治理，再优化检索

Anand给出的第一步是建立可观测与评估体系，尽早发现Context Rot信号。关键指标包括：

- 响应生成时间
- Token消耗量
- 准确率变化
- 推理能力退化趋势

当输入数据持续增加时，若准确率和推理表现持续下滑，就应优先排查上下文质量，而不是盲目加模型或加算力。

在工程实践上，企业需要确保RAG流程中“送进模型的上下文”是高相关、高时效、低冲突的内容，包括文档、工具输出和外部知识源。

## Elastic给出的产品化路径

文章以Elastic方案为例，强调“检索质量+可观测性+语义能力”一体化治理：

- Elasticsearch（分布式搜索与分析）
- 向量数据库与向量存储（embedding管理与召回）
- [Elastic Agent Builder](https://www.elastic.co/docs/explore-analyze/ai-features/elastic-agent-builder)
- [Elastic built-in observability platform](https://www.elastic.co/observability)
- [ELSER](https://www.elastic.co/docs/explore-analyze/machine-learning/nlp/ml-nlp-elser)（语义检索）
- [JINA AI](https://ir.elastic.co/news/news-details/2025/Elastic-Completes-Acquisition-of-Jina-AI-a-Leader-in-Frontier-Models-for-Multimodal-and-Multilingual-Search/default.aspx) 多语种多模态embedding模型

核心目标是：从非结构化数据中召回最相关上下文，并持续监控Agent与LLM表现，避免上下文污染长期累积。

## 分析师观点：Context Rot是LLMOps里的“慢性技术债”

Franconia Research分析师James Kobielus认为，企业必须把Context Rot当作持续治理战，而不是一次性修复。

如果LLMOps管道缺乏强治理，错误、过期、无关上下文会在模型交互中形成恶性循环，导致模型有效性持续下降。对终端用户而言，风险是“无感知误导”——用户不知道系统错了，却被错误答案持续影响。

他建议在RAG工作流前置治理动作：
- 清理不准确、冲突、过期数据与metadata
- 做时间过滤（temporal filtering）
- 做metadata boosting
- 优化数据切块策略
- 校准检索量，匹配Token预算与模型窗口

## 给企业AI团队的落地建议

1. 不要把“更多数据”当成默认优化方向。先看相关性与时效性。  
2. 建立线上评估基线：延迟、Toke

title_en: Under the hood: Security architecture of GitHub Agentic Workflows
title_zh: GitHub Agent工作流安全拆解：4层防线控风险
summary_en: Learn how our threat model and security architecture help teams run agents safely in GitHub Actions.
intro_zh: 这篇文章系统拆解了 GitHub Agentic Workflows 的安全架构：为什么传统 CI/CD 信任模型不够用，以及 GitHub 如何用分层隔离、零秘密暴露、写操作审查和全链路审计来兜底。对正在把 Agent 接入自动化流程的团队来说，核心价值在于可落地的威胁建模思路与工程化防护模式。结论很明确：Agent 要规模化上线，必须先把权限、写入路径和观测能力设计成默认安全。
content_en_preview:
Whether you’re an open-source maintainer or part of an enterprise team, waking up to documentation fixes, new unit tests, and refactoring suggestions can be a true “aha” moment. But automation also raises an important concern: how do you put guardrails on agents that have access to your repository and the internet? Will you be wondering if your agent relied on documentation from a sketchy website, or pushed a commit containing an API token? What if it decides to add noisy comments to every open issue one day? Automations must be predictable to offer durable value.

But what is the safest way to add agents to existing automations like CI/CD? Agents are non-deterministic: They must consume untrusted inputs, reason over repository state, and make decisions at runtime. Letting agents operate in CI/CD without real-time supervision allows you to scale your software engineering, but it also requires novel guardrails to keep you from creating security problems.

GitHub Agentic Workflows run on top of GitHub Actions. By default, everything in an action runs in the same trust domain. Rogue agents can interfere with MCP servers, access authentication secrets, and make network requests to arbitrary hosts. A buggy or prompt-injected agent with unrestricted access to these resources can act in unexpected and insecure ways.

That’s why security is baked into the architecture of GitHub Agentic Workflows. We treat agent execution as an extension of the CI/CD model—not as a separate runtime. We separate open‑ended authoring from governed execution, then compile a workflow into a GitHub Action with explicit constraints such as permissions, outputs, auditability, and network access.

This post explains how we built Agentic Workflows with security in mind from day one, starting with the threat model and the security architecture that it needs.

## Threat model

There are two properties of agentic workflows that change the threat model for automation.

First, agents’ ability to reason ov
content_zh_preview:
当你早上打开仓库，发现文档修好了、单测补齐了、重构建议也准备好了，Agent 自动化的价值会非常直观。

问题也随之而来：这些 Agent 同时接触仓库和互联网，边界怎么划？它会不会引用不可信网页内容？会不会把 API Token 提交进代码？会不会突然给所有 issue 批量刷无意义评论？

要让自动化长期可用，前提不是“更聪明”，而是“可预测、可约束、可审计”。

GitHub Agentic Workflows 基于 GitHub Actions 构建。默认情况下，Action 中组件共享同一信任域；如果 Agent 失控，可能干扰 MCP 服务、触达认证信息、访问任意网络目标。GitHub 的做法是：把 Agent 执行纳入 CI/CD 安全模型，用编译后的受约束工作流替代“自由运行”的 Agent。

本文核心是它的安全设计：从威胁模型出发，落到可执行架构。

## 威胁模型：Agent + 高权限执行环境的组合风险

GitHub 认为，Agent 工作流改变了自动化系统的风险结构，关键在两点：

1. Agent 能理解仓库状态并自主决策，但面对不可信输入时，默认不应被信任。
2. GitHub Actions 的高开放执行环境原本适合确定性自动化；与不可信 Agent 叠加后，单一信任域会放大故障影响面。

在这个模型下，系统默认 Agent 可能会：

- 读写不该访问的状态
- 通过非预期通道通信
- 滥用合法通道执行不受欢迎操作

因此，GitHub Agentic Workflows 默认启用严格安全模式，并遵循四条原则：

- 纵深防御（defense in depth）
- 不让 Agent 接触秘密
- 所有写操作分阶段、可审查
- 全链路记录日志

## 三层安全架构：substrate、configuration、planning

GitHub 采用分层防护，每层限制上一层失效后的影响范围。

![Diagram of a three-layer system architecture with labeled sections Planning layer, Configuration layer, and Substrate layer. Each layer contains three blue tiles:
Planning: Safe Outputs MCP (GitHub write operations), Call filtering (call availability, volume), Output sanitization (secret removal, moderation).
Configuration: Compiler (GH AW extension), Firewall policies (allowlist), MCP config (Docker image, auth token).
Substrate: Action runner VM (OS, hypervisor), Docker containers (Docker daemon, network), Trusted containers (firewall, MCP gateway, API proxy).](https://github.blog/wp-content/uploads/2026/03/image1.jpg?resize=1024%2C949)

### 1) Substrate 层（运行基座隔离）

基于 GitHub Actions runner VM + 多个可信容器，提供：

- 组件隔离
- 特权操作与系统调用中介
- 内核级通信边界

即便某个不可信用户态组件被攻破并执行任意代码，影响也被限制在容器边界内。

### 2) Configuration 层（声明式约束）

这一层决定系统“长什么样、能连什么、拿什么权限”：

- 加载哪些组件
- 组件如何连通
- 允许哪些通信通道
- Token 注入到哪些容器

像 agent API key、GitHub access token 这类外部签发 Token，会被精细绑定到指定容器，限制外部副作用。

### 3) Planning 层（分阶段执行与显式数据交换）

Configuration 只定义“可用结构”，Planning 负责“运行时怎么走”。

其核心是 staged workflow：把执行拆阶段，并定义阶段间显式数据流。后文的 safe outputs 就是这一层的

title_en: How we built LangChain’s GTM Agent
title_zh: LangChain实战：GTM Agent如何带来250%转化提升
summary_en: Learn how we built a GTM agent that increased lead conversion by 250% while saving each sales rep 40 hours per month
intro_zh: 这篇文章拆解了 LangChain 团队如何把销售线索处理从“人肉查资料+手动写邮件”升级为端到端 GTM Agent 流程。核心价值不只在自动化，而在可审计、可解释、可持续学习：既保留人工审批安全阀，又用数据闭环持续提升邮件质量。对正在做 Agent 落地的中国开发者来说，这是一份从业务目标、系统约束到工程实现都很完整的生产级案例。
content_en_preview:
_By Vishnu Suresh and Jess Ou_

Every outbound at LangChain used to start the same way: a rep toggling between tabs. Salesforce for the account record, Gong for call history, LinkedIn for the contact, the company website for context. Fifteen minutes of research before a single word was written, and no easy way to know if a teammate had already reached out yesterday. Inbound follow-up used to mean manually dropping the same message into Apollo for every new contact. 

We built a GTM agent that runs the process end-to-end. It triggers on new Salesforce leads, checks whether we should reach out, gathers context (including meeting history), and sends a Slack draft (with reasoning + sources) for the rep to approve. We built it on [Deep Agents](https://docs.langchain.com/oss/python/deepagents/overview?ref=blog.langchain.com) because this is a long-running, multi-step process that has to orchestrate multiple tools and large amounts of data reliably.

## **Key results**

*   Lead-to-qualified-opportunity conversion rate up 250% from December 2025 to March 2026, driving 3x more pipeline dollars in the same period
*   Since December, reps have increased their follow up with lower intent leads by 97% and higher intent leads by 18%
*   Sales reps reclaimed 40 hours per month each, totaling 1,320 hours across the team
*   50% daily and 86% weekly active usage for sales team members

**Team love**

The GTM agent started as an SDR agent and then became used by the broader GTM team.

content_zh_preview:
_作者：Vishnu Suresh、Jess Ou_

过去在 LangChain，销售外呼基本都是同一套低效流程：
在 Salesforce 看账户、去 Gong 翻通话记录、查 LinkedIn、再看官网背景信息。写第一句话前，常常先花 15 分钟做研究。

更麻烦的是，销售很难快速确认团队里是否已经有人在昨天联系过同一个客户。对于 inbound 线索，跟进也常常是把同一段话手动贴进 Apollo 发给每个联系人。

我们最终做了一个端到端 GTM Agent：
它在 Salesforce 出现新线索时自动触发，先判断“要不要联系”，再拉取上下文（包括会议历史），最后把带有推理依据和信息来源的草稿发到 Slack，由销售审批。

之所以选择 [Deep Agents](https://docs.langchain.com/oss/python/deepagents/overview?ref=blog.langchain.com)，是因为这是一个长流程、多步骤、强依赖多工具与大数据量编排的任务，需要高可靠执行。

## 结果先看：业务指标变化

*   2025 年 12 月到 2026 年 3 月，线索到合格商机转化率提升 250%，同期 pipeline 金额达到 3 倍
*   自 12 月以来，低意向线索跟进提升 97%，高意向线索跟进提升 18%
*   每位销售每月节省 40 小时，全团队累计回收 1,320 小时
*   销售团队日活 50%，周活 86%

**团队反馈**

这个 GTM Agent 最初是 SDR Agent，之后扩展到更广泛的 GTM 团队使用。

![](https://blog.langchain.com/content/images/2026/03/data-src-image-bd0d930c-3f79-45ec-a18f-7f61dcee8f4f.png)

![](https://blog.langchain.com/content/images/2026/03/data-src-image-8135b29f-2698-4512-b56f-7dc099c75bc9.png)

![](https://blog.langchain.com/content/images/2026/03/data-src-image-4474bd1f-1954-4ea6-81e6-1ff770264f37.png)

![](https://blog.langchain.com/content/images/2026/03/data-src-image-c5528a22-ecdd-4d21-a90d-1a4e869bd568.png)

![](https://blog.langchain.com/content/images/2026/03/data-src-image-5729db30-b848-48a9-b3aa-fcc0745c3206.png)

![](https://blog.langchain.com/content/images/2026/03/data-src-image-4472e2ac-9cd2-48ec-a53b-cb2d8e84e8fa.png)

## 设计前先定边界：约束与成功标准

写代码前，我们先定义清楚 Agent 必须完成什么。

目标只有两个：
1) 降低销售在每条线索上的研究和写作时间；
2) 提升营销导入线索（inbound）的转化。

外呼研究和草稿生成是可自动化的，但前提是系统必须安全、可审计，并且能越用越好。

#### 不可妥协的原则

*   **Human-in-the-loop：** 没有销售明确审批，任何内容都不能自动发出。一次时机错误的邮件，可能破坏几个月建立的关系。
*   **联系历史感知：** 生成草稿前，必须检查是否已有本人或队友联系过该客户。

#### 核心能力要求

*   **关系状态驱动的个性化：** 草稿要体现账户当前关系阶段（客户 / 温线索 / 冷线索），不能“一套模板打天下”。
*   **可解释性：** 销售需要看到关键输入和 Agent 选择该切入角度的原因，才能有效修订与反馈。
*   **学习闭环：** Agent 要能从销售编辑中持续学习，而不是每次靠人工改 prompt。

#### 如何衡量

销售每次动作（发送、编辑、取消）都会记录到 [LangSmith](https://www.langchain.com/langsmith-platform?ref=blog.langch

title_en: Cursor builds always-on agents to tackle developer task tedium
title_zh: Cursor上线常驻Agent：把代码审查与排障全自动化
summary_en: Future workflows will see boosted productivity with Cursor Automations: AI agents that handle code reviews, bug fixes & triage inside a developer's codebase.
intro_zh: Cursor 发布了可“常驻运行”的 Automations，让 Agent 按事件或定时自动处理代码审查、故障分诊、死代码清理等重复工作。对中国开发者来说，这不只是新功能，而是研发流程从“人驱动”走向“Agent 驱动”的信号。核心价值在于：把高频、低创造性的工程劳动交给 Agent，人类开发者把精力放在架构与关键决策上。
content_en_preview:
AI-powered code editor company [Cursor](https://cursor.com/get-started) has introduced Cursor Automations to build [always-on agents](https://thenewstack.io/ai-agents-and-their-life-cycle-what-you-should-know/) that operate on its deep understanding of the codebase.

Established as a fork of Microsoft’s extensible VS Code code editor, Cursor is known for its Plan Mode, which helps developers create detailed implementation plans before writing any code; its Composer interface, which allows users to build features based on natural-language instructions; and its Tab predictive autocomplete functionality.

As agents themselves have become increasingly smart, the Cursor team says that they found themselves “kicking them off for the same kind of task over and over again,” so, logically, they thought, why not automate that process too?

## Dead code cleanup

Example use cases include an incident triage process that run everytime a PagerDuty monitor goes off, or a personal automation that looks at all a developer’s pull requests on a given day and then works to clean up dead code or bad programming patterns (such as deeply nested conditionals where an excess of if-then statements hide core system logic) to make developers’ lives easier.

Cursor’s engineering team member, [Jack Pertschuk,](https://www.linkedin.com/in/jack-pertschuk-833196114/) and Cursor software engineer, [Jon Kaplan,](https://www.linkedin.com/in/jkap/) explain more of the rationale behind Cursor’s genesis and its latest augmentations in a [video](https://cursor.com/blog/automations) released on March 5.

> “All those things that people are used to configuring with checkboxes don’t have to be done anymore.”

“The way we have changed software application development has changed so much in the last nine months — and with more software and more output, there’s more stuff that you need to review, more issues you need [to triage and more things to manage around software](https://thenewstack.io/running-more-low-s
content_zh_preview:
AI 编码编辑器 [Cursor](https://cursor.com/get-started) 发布了 Cursor Automations：可持续运行的 Agent，基于对代码库上下文的深度理解，自动处理重复开发任务。

Cursor 起步于 Microsoft VS Code 分支，之前已经有几个标志性能力：
- Plan Mode：先做实现方案，再写代码
- Composer：用自然语言生成功能
- Tab：预测式自动补全

团队发现，很多 Agent 任务会被反复手动触发，既然模式固定，就可以进一步自动化。

## 典型场景：从故障分诊到死代码清理

Cursor 给出的示例包括：
- PagerDuty 告警触发后自动执行 incident triage
- 每天扫描开发者当天所有 PR，清理 dead code
- 识别并修复糟糕模式，例如过深条件嵌套（大量 if-then 掩盖核心逻辑）

Cursor 工程团队成员 [Jack Pertschuk](https://www.linkedin.com/in/jack-pertschuk-833196114/) 和软件工程师 [Jon Kaplan](https://www.linkedin.com/in/jkap/) 在 3 月 5 日发布的[视频](https://cursor.com/blog/automations)中解释了这套设计思路。

> “All those things that people are used to configuring with checkboxes don’t have to be done anymore.”

他们的判断是：近 9 个月软件开发方式变化很大，产出更多，待审查与待管理事项也同步增加，而其中大量工作天然适合自动化——不再依赖繁琐配置，直接 prompt Agent 即可。

## 触发机制：定时 + 事件驱动

Automations 支持两类触发方式：
- 定时执行
- 事件触发

可直接接入的事件源包括：
- Slack 消息
- Linear 新建 issue
- GitHub PR 合并
- PagerDuty incident

除内置集成外，也可通过 webhook 定义自定义事件。

Agent 被触发后，会在云端沙箱环境中启动，按开发者配置的模型与 [Model Context Protocol](https://thenewstack.io/model-context-protocol-a-primer-for-the-developers/)（MCP）连接执行任务，并对输出进行自检。系统还提供记忆工具，让 Agent 从历史运行中学习，反复执行越做越稳。

## 代码审查自动化正在变“细而全”

Cursor 将自动化重点放在 code review：
- 小问题：代码风格 nits、一致性问题
- 大问题：安全漏洞、性能回退

文中提到的 [Cursor Bugbot](https://cursor.com/bugbot)（2025 年 6 月随 Cursor 1.0 推出）可在每个 PR 上自动审查 bug、安全与代码质量，被称为“最初的 automation”。

随着平台能力增强，用户现在可以为不同目标定制不同审查 Agent，而不是依赖单一规则集。

## Cursor 自用实践：把流程跑成日常机制

Cursor 在公告中披露了内部使用方式（dogfooding）：
- **安全审查自动化**：每次 push 到 main 即触发；审计 diff 中的漏洞；跳过 PR 中已讨论问题；高风险结果推送到 Slack。官方称其已捕获多个漏洞和关键 bug。
- **每日例行审查**：每天自动检查近期合并代码，找出测试覆盖盲区；按现有约定补测试，仅在必要时调整生产行为；运行相关测试后再发起 PR。
- **每周仓库摘要**：自动发送 Slack digest，总结过去 7 天的重要 PR、bug 修复、技术债、安全与依赖更新。

这套节奏说明：自动化不只是“帮你写代码”，而是深入研发治理流程。

## 行业含义：不是替代开发者，而是重排分工

文末也回应了常见疑问：这类工具会终结软件开发吗？结论相反——它更像是放大工程师产能。

Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang 在 Cursor 网站引用中表示，Nvidia 约 40,000 名工程师都在 AI 辅助下工作，生产力显著提升。

title_en: Andrew Ng × Anthropic「Agent Skills」Course Guide

summary_en: A comprehensive guide to DeepLearning.AI's Agent Skills with Anthropic course: lesson breakdown, practical tools, and learning path.
content_en_preview:
## Why This Course Matters

In January 2026, Andrew Ng partnered with Anthropic to launch a free short course on DeepLearning.AI — Agent Skills with Anthropic, taught by Elie Schoppik, Anthropic's Head of Technical Education.

Agent Skills are an open standard for packaging reusable instructions that extend AI agent capabilities. This 2-hour course covers the full spectrum: from concept to Claude Code, Claude API, and Agent SDK integration.

## Course Overview

10 lessons covering: Skill structure and progressive disclosure, pre-built vs custom Skills, Skills vs MCP vs Subagents, API integration, Claude Code workflows, and multi-agent systems with Agent SDK.

Total duration: ~2h 19min | Level: Beginner | Free
content_zh_preview:
## 为什么这门课值得关注

2026 年 1 月，吴恩达联合 Anthropic 在 DeepLearning.AI 上线了一门免费短课程——[Agent Skills with Anthropic](https://www.deeplearning.ai/short-courses/agent-skills-with-anthropic/)。讲师是 Anthropic 技术教育负责人 Elie Schoppik。

三个关键词解释了这门课的价值：

- **Agent Skills 是开放标准**——不是 Anthropic 的私有格式，OpenAI Codex CLI、Cursor、VS Code 都已支持
- **2 小时可完成**——10 节课，从概念到 Claude Code、Claude API、Agent SDK 全覆盖
- **免费**——零门槛，适合所有想让 AI Agent 变得更专业的开发者

> 如果你用过 Claude Code 但从没写过 Skill，这门课是最快的入门路径。如果你已经在写 Skill，第 7-10 课的进阶内容值得一看。

---

## 课程概览

| # | 课时 | 时长 | 核心内容 |
|---|------|------|---------|
| 1 | Introduction | 2 min | Skills 定义与课程路线图 |
| 2 | Course Materials | 1 min | 配套代码和阅读材料 |
| 3 | Why Use Skills - Part I | 11 min | Skill 文件夹结构、渐进式披露、Excel Skill 案例拆解 |
| 4 | Why Use Skills - Part II | 8 min | Agent 类型与 Skill 方向映射 |
| 5 | Skills vs Tools, MCP, and Subagents | 7 min | 三者定位对比：SOP vs 感知器 vs 分身 |
| 6 | Exploring Pre-Built Skills | 18 min | 官方预构建 Skill、Skill Creator、最佳实践 |
| 7 | Creating Custom Skills | 16 min | SKILL.md 规范、命名规则、两个自定义 Skill 实战 |
| 8 | Skills with the Claude API | 17 min | Code Execution Tool + Files API + Skill 集成 |
| 9 | Skills with Claude Code | 24 min | Claude Code 工作流 + Subagent + Skill 协作 |
| 10 | Skills with the Claude Agent SDK | 20 min | 多智能体研究 Agent + MCP 集成 + Notion 交付 |

**总时长**: ~2 小时 19 分钟 | **难度**: 初学者 | **代码仓库**: [sc-agent-skills-files](https://github.com/https-deeplearning-ai/sc-agent-skills-files)

---

## 逐课导读

> 第 1-2 课为课程介绍和材料准备（共 3 分钟），以下从第 3 课的实质内容开始导读。

### 第 3 课：为什么用 Skills（上）

**核心概念**——Skill 是一个文件夹，包含指令（Instructions）、脚本（Scripts）和资源（Assets），让 Agent 获得特定领域的专业能力。

课程用一个 **Excel 营销分析 Skill** 做演示：没有 Skill 时，你每次都要重复描述数据格式、分析方法、输出模板；有 Skill 后，Agent 自动知道怎么做。

**关键机制——渐进式披露（Progressive Disclosure）**：

```
第 1 层: name + description → 始终加载到上下文（几十个 token）
第 2 层: SKILL.md 正文 → Agent 判断相关时才加载
第 3 层: scripts/ references/ assets/ → 按需加载
```

这个设计解决了"加载太多指令浪费 token"的问题。Agent 只在需要时才读取完整指令。

**SkillNav 推荐**：浏览我们的[数据处理类 Skills](/skills?category=数据处理) 查看类似的数据分析 Skill 实例。
